tasks.Name: Email DMARC Policy
tasks.pageText:
Here is the result of "view" for the Page with URL https://app.notion.com/p/37e0f43189ea8052847de68fe04f346b as of 2026-06-13T18:27:52.999Z:
<page url="https://app.notion.com/p/37e0f43189ea8052847de68fe04f346b">
<ancestor-path>
<parent-data-source url="collection://3240f431-89ea-810b-bd74-000b40daec05" name="Myndral Kanban"/>
<ancestor-2-database url="https://app.notion.com/p/3240f43189ea80929806e23c0267068d" title=""/>
</ancestor-path>
<properties>
{"Description":"","Name":"Email DMARC Policy","Status":"To Do","Team":"Index","date:Deadline:is_datetime":0,"url":"https://app.notion.com/p/37e0f43189ea8052847de68fe04f346b"}
</properties>
<content>
This is a DMARC aggregate report — Google sends these automatically to the `rua` address in your DMARC DNS record when it processes email claiming to be from `minkops.com`. Here's what it tells you.
**What DMARC checks**
For each email, DMARC evaluates two sub-checks: DKIM (cryptographic signature) and SPF (envelope sender IP authorization). To pass DMARC, at least one of these must both pass AND be aligned with your `header_from` domain (minkops.com).
**What happened across 5 emails (2 + 3)**
Both records show the same pattern, originating from `209.85.220.41` (a legitimate Google outbound mail server):
- DKIM: **pass** — your minkops.com Google Workspace key (selector: `google`) signed the messages correctly. Alignment with `header_from: minkops.com` is satisfied.
- SPF: **fail at policy level** — the envelope sender (Return-Path / MAIL FROM) domain was `minkowskihome.com`, not `minkops.com`. SPF returned `none` for `minkowskihome.com`, meaning that domain has no SPF record published at all.
**Why this is happening**
The envelope sender being `minkowskihome.com` while the header-from is `minkops.com` strongly suggests one of:
- Google Workspace bounce/return routing is leaking your Minkowski Home domain configuration into Minkops outbound mail, or
- Some sending path (alias, forwarding rule, or shared Workspace account) is using the wrong identity.
Record 1 also shows a DKIM signature from `minkowskihome-com.20251104.gappssmtp.com`, which confirms cross-domain leakage — a Minkowski Home Google Workspace signing identity is appearing on Minkops mail.
**Is this a problem right now?**
No immediate damage. Because DKIM passes with minkops.com alignment, DMARC passes overall. Your policy is also `p=none`, so even a DMARC failure would only be reported, not acted on.
**What you should fix**
1. **Add an SPF record for minkowskihome.com** regardless of whether you're actively sending from it. The `none` result means the domain is unprotected. A minimal record: `v=spf1 include:_spf.google.com ~all` if it's on Google Workspace.
2. **Investigate the sending path causing the envelope mismatch.** Check your Google Workspace Admin console under Apps \> Google Workspace \> Gmail \> Routing to see if any rules or aliases are causing Minkops outbound mail to carry a Minkowski Home return-path.
3. **Long term:** Once email is clean, tighten your DMARC policy from `p=none` to `p=quarantine` and eventually `p=reject`. You're currently in monitoring mode only.
The core signal is your two Google Workspace domains (minkops.com and minkowskihome.com) are leaking into each other's mail flow. Since MH is paused, the priority is just ensuring it has a valid SPF record and isn't contaminating Minkops outbound identity.
<empty-block/>
</content>
</page>

	- [ ] The current version is very makeshift and not user friendly — uncategorized jumbled items on navbar menu, no way to cleanly view the existing catalogue, poor UX. Think about all the aspects of easy of use and brainstorm a way to convert it into an industry grade catalogue management system for Myndral-like apps.
	- [ ] The Internal studio app needs to expose a Media tab too — where you can view/manage/replace all media in GCS, live or not, like artist photos, audio uploads (including songs — this is not redundant with the songs tab and is intentional), album arts, media in feed posts, merch media etc. Study the schema carefully, think deeply about what all fields to expose to give full control and optimal ease of use to the editor.

Study the schema carefully, think deeply about what all fields to expose to give full control and optimal ease of use to the editor.
Think deeply, this is a one-shot pass and need a useable CMS ready asap. Ask any clarifying questions before beginning.

tasks.Name: Automation Frameworks
tasks.pageText:
Here is the result of "view" for the Page with URL https://app.notion.com/p/3540f43189ea80abb77ee58d09eed851 as of 2026-05-15T15:18:41.737Z:
<page url="https://app.notion.com/p/3540f43189ea80abb77ee58d09eed851">
<ancestor-path>
<parent-data-source url="collection://3240f431-89ea-810b-bd74-000b40daec05" name="Myndral Kanban"/>

<properties>
{"Description":"","Name":"Automation Frameworks","Status":"Done","Team":"Operations","date:Deadline:is_datetime":0,"url":"https://app.notion.com/p/3540f43189ea80abb77ee58d09eed851"}
</properties>
<content>
- Document every function as if you were hiring someone to do it. You can’t automate what you haven’t documented.
- Break ALL functions (tech functions as well as social accounts and email aliases) into designated roles as if you’re filling some person’s seat, instead of doing everything in a cluttered fashion.
	- Kartik (engineering), Sonal (user-facing/marketing), Varun (features/AI) + other functional roles. Assign an email alias or some social account to each — these mimic real humans that stay consistent across the universe, create a company of employees that you’ll play. By now, you have a fixed size team with MECE functions — each member with properly assigned email aliases and social accounts etc. Only then move to next step.
	1. Map your seats — CEO, Engineer, Marketing, Sales, Support, HR. Each gets one primary metric (e.g., Sales = pipeline conversations/week).
	2. For each seat, run it manually for 2–4 weeks and document the process (E-Myth SOP logic).
	3. Rank seats by: (a) frequency of task recurrence and (b) how rule-based vs. judgment-based the work is. High frequency + rule-based = automate first.
	4. Automate in this proven order: Support → Marketing ops → Sales outreach → Reporting. Engineering and CEO judgment seats stay with you longest.
</content>
</page>

<content>
### Media Best Practices
- [ ] Clean the Google Drive - try to make docs in-repo and version-controlled as possible. Get Rid of Drive docs.
	- [ ] Items like Albums sheets or Artists Directory, are only needed for human readability — they should eventually be served through **Internal Tool **(likely the Studio app itself) and retrieve from the production database itself
	- [ ] Images and Media, also, must live on GCS — Drive is a collaboration tool, not source-of-truth storage. The industry standard is object storage (S3, GCS, Azure Blob) as the canonical store for original/master files, with a CDN in front for delivery. 
	- [ ] A CDN (Cloudflare, Fastly, or Cloud CDN if you're already on GCP) sits in front and serves transformed/resized variants to the application. Image transformation can be handled by a service like Imgix, Cloudflare Images, or Thumbor — you request `image.jpg?w=400` and the CDN caches the resized version. This is also standard.
- [ ] Ditch the current brand bible and use **shared/catalogue/universe_bible_canon.json **reconstruct but with a better design consistent with other docs.
- [ ] Obsidian \> Job Prep \> Interviews, etc. \> Interview Stories \> Myndral Proxy Problem
</content>

Library — Implicit Saves - [x] Save a track (heart it from any album page) - [x] Go to **Library → Saved → Albums**: the album that track belongs to appears with subtitle **"via saved song"** and the album's artist appears with subtitle **"Artist via saved music"** - [x] Items that you explicitly saved (by clicking Save Artist / Save Album directly) show the old subtitles ("Saved artist", etc.) - [x] **Implicit album link**: clicking an implicit album opens the album page with only the saved track(s) visible — a notice "Showing only your saved songs from this album." appears - [x] Play button on an implicit album context plays only the visible (saved) tracks, not the whole album - [x] **Implicit artist link**: clicking an implicit artist opens the artist page with section titles changed to "Saved Songs" and "Saved Albums", filtered accordingly — a notice appears under each section - No separate sections titles appear - [x] Remove the saved track → the implicit album and artist **disappear** from Library (cleanup) - [x] If you later explicitly save the same artist, they appear with the normal subtitle even after removing the track ### 4. Create Artist — Band / Group Support - [x] Open **Create Artist** panel - [x] "Artist type" radio group is visible: **Individual** / **Band / Group** - [x] Default selection is Individual; band-member fields are hidden - [x] Selecting **Band** reveals at least two member-artist dropdowns - [x] The **"+ Add member"** button appends another dropdown row - [x] Each dropdown is populated with existing (non-archived) artists - [x] Submitting a band with **fewer than 2 members selected** shows the validation error: "A band must have at least 2 member artists selected." - [x] Submitting with ≥ 2 members creates the artist successfully - [x] The preview step shows "Artist type: Band / Group" and lists the member names - [x] **Editing** an existing band artist pre-populates the member dropdowns correctly - [x] Switching back to Individual hides member fields and clears them before submit ### 7. Image Input — Draggable Crop UI - [x] When uploading any artist, album, or track cover image, the crop UI appears - The crop UI appears correctly, but provides aspect ratio options — the aspect ratio should be locked per image type (portrait or cover photo — one aspect ratio each) - Even after cancelling before upload, the image shows uploaded in the UI - [x] An initial crop rectangle is shown, centered and filling the image as much as possible at the correct aspect ratio - [x] **Dragging the crop rectangle** by its interior moves it; it stays within the image bounds - [x] **Dragging a corner handle** (nw / ne / sw / se) resizes the rectangle; it cannot go below the minimum size (\~50 px) - [x] The crop cannot be dragged or resized outside the image edges - [x] Changing the **aspect ratio preset** resets the crop rectangle to the new ratio, centered - [x] The final saved image reflects the crop (not the full uncropped image) - [x] Rapid corner-dragging outside the image element then releasing the mouse does not leave the drag in a stuck state ### 8. <content>
Search for and clean up
- [ ] Features will work, users will be happy, but the codebase will be a mess, unmaintainable, and onboarding new engineers would be impossible. Take extreme care of code-debt.
- [ ] ‼️ Issue items in Obsidian Build Logs
- [ ] Trailing memory-eating items in GCS
- [ ] Basic Security like Hardcoding keys etc
- [ ] API key compromise protection tactics
- [ ] RLS
- [ ] Public bucket URLs and bucket safety
- [ ] Code duplication / Code bloat
- [ ] SEO sanity
- [ ] Unnecessary coupling
- [ ] Coding anti patterns 
- [ ] Bad system design 
- [ ] Unoptimized images, fonts, and bundles
- [ ] Understand and Read all code before shipping - zero code debt
---
- [x] Opening the sign up screen / sign in screen (app.myndral.com) takes too long sometimes — check if it’s a problem or everything is normal
- [x] Image loading, opening pages, etc. is slow in general too — aggresively test against system design concepts and improve performance.
- [ ]  When scaling catalogue: the biggest long-term risk here is client-side filtering over server-paginated results. It is okay short-term, but at “thousands of records” scale it can produce incomplete filtered discovery and confusing totals. May be convert these filters to server-side query params so behavior scales cleanly and predictably.
- [ ] Read the `docs/security-audits/` and solve them (Read first before blindly passing to Codex, not all issues need addressing immediately).
</content>
</page>

<content>
## Android Manual Testing Checklist — `feature/android-review-ready`
> **Note:** Brand-new native Kotlin + Jetpack Compose app. Build and install via Android Studio or `./gradlew installDebug`. Use the same dev credentials as the other platforms.
---
### 1. Auth — Login Screen
- [ ] App opens to the Login screen (no blank/crash on cold start)
- [ ] "Myndral" wordmark renders in the brand primary colour
- [ ] Username and password fields accept input; password is masked
- [ ] Both fields must be non-empty for the Sign In button to be enabled
- [ ] Tapping Sign In while loading shows "Signing in..." label and disables the button (no double-submit)
- [ ] A circular progress indicator overlays the screen while the login request is in flight
- [ ] Bad credentials show an inline red error message below the fields
- [ ] Successful login navigates to the Home tab; Back does not return to Login
- [ ] "Don't have an account? Register" link navigates to the Register screen
### 2. Auth — Register Screen
- [ ] All four fields present: Display Name (optional), Username, Email, Password
- [ ] Register button requires Username, Email, and Password to be non-empty (Display Name is optional)
- [ ] "Creating account..." label and disabled button while request is in flight
- [ ] Registration error renders in red inline
- [ ] Successful registration navigates forward (to ChoosePlan or Home)
- [ ] "Already have an account? Sign In" link returns to Login
### 3. Auth — Choose Plan Screen (Onboarding)
- [ ] Three plan cards present: Free, Premium Monthly (`$9.99/month`), Premium Annual (`$79.99/year · Save 33%`)
- [ ] Tapping a card selects it — border turns to primary colour, check icon appears, background shifts to `primaryDim`
- [ ] Free is selected by default
- [ ] Each card lists the correct feature bullet points with green check icons
- [ ] Button label is **"Continue for Free"** when Free is selected, **"Get Started"** for premium plans
- [ ] Tapping Continue navigates into the app (billing is UI-only at this stage — no actual payment flow expected)
### 4. Home Tab
- [ ] Time-of-day greeting ("Good morning/afternoon/evening, DisplayName.") renders correctly
- [ ] Loading spinner + "Loading your feed..." shows while data is fetching
- [ ] Once loaded, the following sections each render only when non-empty: **New Releases** (album cards), **Featured Artists** (artist cards), **Trending Tracks** (track rows), **Artists** (artist cards), **Albums** (album cards), **Songs** (track rows), **Playlists** (playlist list items)
- [ ] Album cards and artist cards are horizontal `LazyRow` rails — they scroll independently
- [ ] Tapping an album card navigates to Album Detail
- [ ] Tapping an artist card navigates to Artist Detail
- [ ] Tapping a playlist row navigates to Playlist Detail
- [ ] Tapping a track row starts playback and triggers the Mini Player
### 5. Search Tab
- [ ] Search field renders with a magnifier icon; placeholder text "Songs, albums, artists…"
- [ ] Empty or blank query shows the "Search Myndral" empty state (no results rendered)
- [ ] Typing shows a loading spinner while the debounced query is in flight
- [ ] Results render in four sections: **Songs**, **Albums**, **Artists**, **Playlists** — each only when non-empty
- [ ] Track rows in results show album title in the subtitle (`showAlbum = true`)
- [ ] Pressing a track row starts playback; Mini Player appears
- [ ] Pressing an album or artist card navigates to the respective detail screen
- [ ] A query that matches nothing shows "No results — Nothing matched '…'. Try different keywords."
- [ ] Clearing the field returns to the empty state
### 6. Library Tab
- [ ] "Your Library" heading renders
- [ ] **Segmented control** switches between "Saved" and "Favorites" — the active tab highlights in `primaryDim` with primary-coloured text
- [ ] **"Create Playlist"** button is present and opens the playlist creation bottom sheet (see §14)
- [ ] Loading spinner shows while data is fetching
### Saved view
- [ ] Four sections visible: Artists, Albums, Playlists, Songs — each with an item count badge
- [ ] Empty section shows the "No saved X yet." placeholder text, not a crash or missing section
- [ ] **Artist row** for an explicitly saved artist shows subtitle "Saved artist"; tapping navigates to Artist Detail
- [ ] **Artist row** for an implicitly-saved artist (pulled in by saving a track) shows subtitle "Artist via saved music"; tapping navigates to Artist Detail
- [ ] **Album row** for an explicitly saved album shows `"ArtistName · N songs"`; tapping navigates to Album Detail
- [ ] **Album row** for an implicitly-saved album shows `"ArtistName · via saved music"`; tapping navigates to Album Detail
- [ ] **Playlist row** tapping navigates to Playlist Detail
- [ ] **Song rows** support the full track row interactions (see §13)
### Favorites view
- [ ] Three sections: Favorite Artists, Favorite Albums, Favorite Songs
- [ ] Tapping a favorite artist/album navigates to the detail screen
- [ ] Favorite song rows support playback and overflow actions
### 7. Feed Tab
- [ ] "Feed" heading and "Latest posts and reels from Myndral artists." subtitle render
- [ ] Refresh icon button in the top-right reloads the post list
- [ ] Loading spinner shows on first load; error state renders if the API fails
- [ ] "No posts yet" empty state renders when there are no live posts
- [ ] Each post card shows: circular artist avatar, artist name, post type label ("Post" or "Reel")
- [ ] The **first post** in the list shows a "New" badge in primary colour
- [ ] A post with an **image** media URL renders the image at full width (320 dp height)
- [ ] A post with a **video** media URL renders a placeholder box (260 dp) with a film-reel icon and "Video reel" label — no actual video playback on Android
- [ ] A post with **no media** renders only the caption (no blank box or crash)
- [ ] Caption text renders below the media area
- [ ] If the post has a linked track, `"Audio: Track Title · Artist Name"` renders in primary colour below the caption
- [ ] **Like button** (heart icon): tapping toggles filled/outline icon and updates the count immediately (optimistic)
- [ ] **Comment count** renders as a read-only bubble icon + number (no comment drawer on Android)
- [ ] **Share button**: tapping opens the Android system share sheet with `https://app.myndral.com/feed?post=<id>`
### 8. Album Detail Screen
- [ ] Back arrow navigates back to the previous screen
- [ ] Loading spinner shows while album is fetching
- [ ] Square album artwork renders at full width with rounded corners
- [ ] Album title, artist name (tappable — navigates to Artist Detail), release year, album type, and genre tags all render
- [ ] **"Play"** button starts playback from the first track; disabled if no tracks have audio URLs
- [ ] **"Export"** button triggers the export flow (check a confirmation message or download queued toast)
- [ ] **"Add to Library" / "Saved"** button toggles the saved state; label changes after tap
- [ ] **"Favorite" / "Favorited"** button toggles the favorite state; label changes after tap
- [ ] Error messages and confirmation messages appear below the action buttons
- [ ] "Tracks" section header renders; track list shows all tracks with index numbers
- [ ] Tapping a track row starts playback with the album as the queue
### 9. Artist Detail Screen
- [ ] Back arrow navigates back
- [ ] Loading spinner shows while artist is fetching
- [ ] Hero image renders at full width (240 dp height) with rounded corners
- [ ] Artist name, monthly listener count (formatted with commas), bio, and style tags render
- [ ] **"Play Artist"** button starts playback from top tracks; disabled when no top tracks have audio
- [ ] **"Add to Library" / "Saved"** button toggles library state
- [ ] **"Favorite" / "Favorited"** button toggles favorite state; full-width button
- [ ] Error text appears in red when an action fails
- [ ] **"Top Tracks"** section lists tracks with album names visible
- [ ] **"Discography"** section is a horizontal scroll rail of album cards; tapping navigates to Album Detail
### 10. Mini Player
- [ ] Mini Player appears above the bottom navigation bar after any track starts playing
- [ ] Shows: album artwork thumbnail (40×40 dp, rounded), track title (truncated), artist name (truncated)
- [ ] **Play/Pause** icon button toggles playback in place
- [ ] **Next** (skip) icon button advances to the next track in the queue
- [ ] A thin linear progress bar fills from left to right at the bottom of the mini player
- [ ] Tapping anywhere on the Mini Player (except the icon buttons) opens the full-screen Player
- [ ] Mini Player disappears when nothing is playing
### 11. Full-screen Player
- [ ] "Now Playing" label and a chevron-down button appear at the top; tapping the chevron collapses back to the previous screen
- [ ] "Nothing is playing" empty state shows when no track is loaded
- [ ] Album artwork renders at full width in a `GlassSurface` card (1:1 aspect ratio, rounded corners)
- [ ] Track title and `"Artist · Album"` subtitle render below the artwork
- [ ] **"Buffering…"** text appears in primary colour while the stream is loading
- [ ] **Progress slider** is draggable — seeking to a different position updates playback
- [ ] Elapsed time (left) and total duration (right) render in `m:ss` format below the slider
- [ ] **Shuffle** icon: tapping toggles shuffle on/off; icon tints to primary when on
- [ ] **Previous** (⏮): tapping skips to the previous track in the queue
- [ ] **Play/Pause** (large, 64 dp): toggles playback; icon switches between filled circle play and pause
- [ ] **Next** (⏭): advances to the next track in the queue
- [ ] **Repeat** icon cycles through none → all → one; icon tints primary when active; `RepeatOne` icon appears in "one" mode
- [ ] **Volume slider** is draggable and adjusts system/stream volume
- [ ] Queue advances automatically when a track finishes (respecting repeat/shuffle mode)
### 12. Track Row — Overflow Menu & Actions
Every `TrackRow` in Home, Search, Library, Album Detail, and Artist Detail should support:
- [ ] Tapping the row body starts playback
- [ ] **Favorite icon** (heart): tapping toggles filled/outline immediately; state is persisted after navigating away and returning
- [ ] **Overflow button (⋮)**: tapping opens a bottom sheet with options
- [ ] Bottom sheet options: **Play Next**, **Add to Queue**, **Favorite/Unfavorite**, **Save/Remove from Library**, **Export/Download**, **Add to Playlist**
- [ ] "Play Next" inserts the track after the current track in the queue (verify in the full-screen player queue)
- [ ] "Add to Queue" appends the track to the end of the queue
- [ ] "Save/Remove from Library" toggles the library state and updates the icon
- [ ] "Export/Download" shows a confirmation message ("Export queued in Downloads." or similar)
- [ ] "Add to Playlist" opens the playlist picker sheet (see §14)
- [ ] Action errors surface as inline messages inside the bottom sheet
### 13. Playlist Creation & Track Addition
- [ ] Tapping **"Create Playlist"** in Library opens a bottom sheet with: Name field, Description field, Public/Private toggle
- [ ] Submitting with a name creates the playlist and closes the sheet; it appears in the Library Playlists section after refresh
- [ ] "Add to Playlist" from the track overflow opens a list of editable playlists
- [ ] Selecting a playlist adds the track and closes the sheet with a "Added to playlist." confirmation
### 14. Account Tab
- [ ] "Account" heading renders
- [ ] **Profile card**: circular avatar with the first letter of the display name, full display name, `@username`, and subscription plan label in primary colour
- [ ] **Appearance section**: three theme rows — Light, Dark, "Minkowski Paper (Premium)" (when user is free plan)
- [ ] The currently active theme row shows a filled primary dot on the right; others are unselected
- [ ] Tapping **Light** or **Dark** switches the app theme immediately — background, surfaces, text colours all update
- [ ] The **Paper** row is disabled (not clickable) for free-plan users; the label shows "(Premium)"
- [ ] **Creator Studio section** (when user has `content_editor`, `content_reviewer`, or `admin` role): shows "Open Studio" button navigating into Studio screens
- [ ] **Creator Studio section** (regular listener role): shows "Claim Studio Access" and a description; tapping navigates to the Studio Access screen
- [ ] **"Sign Out"** button (danger/red colour) logs out, clears stored credentials, and navigates to the Login screen — pressing Back does not return to the app
### 15. Studio — Artists Screen
- [ ] Status filter chips ("All", Draft, Review, Published, Archived) filter the artist list
- [ ] FAB (+) opens a create-artist bottom sheet
- [ ] Creating an artist with valid data adds it to the list in Draft/Review status
- [ ] Each artist card shows a `StatusBadge` with the correct status colour
- [ ] Edit and revoke actions work on existing artists
### 16. Studio — Albums Screen
- [ ] Status filter chips filter the album list
- [ ] FAB (+) opens a create-album bottom sheet
- [ ] Album creation requires a linked artist; the artist picker works correctly
- [ ] StatusBadge reflects the correct state
### 17. Studio — Songs Screen
- [ ] Status filter chips filter the track list
- [ ] FAB (+) opens a create-track (song) bottom sheet
- [ ] Track creation requires a linked album; the album picker works correctly
- [ ] StatusBadge reflects the correct state
### 18. Studio — Staging Screen
- [ ] Three sections render when content is pending: **Artists**, **Albums**, **Tracks** — each with a count in the header
- [ ] "Queue is empty" empty state renders when nothing is pending
- [ ] Refresh button reloads the queue
- [ ] **Approve** button on an artist/album/track card: moves it out of the queue and publishes it
- [ ] **Reject** button removes it from the queue
- [ ] Approved content becomes visible on the consumer-facing tabs after navigating to Home or Search
### 19. Theme — Light / Dark
- [ ] Switching between Light and Dark from Account applies immediately across all tabs and the player
- [ ] Color token values match the design: background, surface, glass panels, primary accent (orange), text hierarchy
- [ ] Status bar / system bar colour adapts to the active theme
### 20. Cross-cutting / Regression Checks
- [ ] **Playback continuity**: start a track on Home, navigate through all tabs — audio keeps playing; Mini Player stays visible
- [ ] **Session persistence**: force-stop and reopen the app — user is still logged in; Home loads without re-authenticating
- [ ] **Implicit save cleanup**: save a track (from the overflow menu), verify the album and artist appear implicitly in Library; remove the track from library and verify they disappear
- [ ] **Back navigation**: all detail screens (Album, Artist) return correctly via the Back button and the Android system back gesture
- [ ] **No crash on rapid navigation**: quickly switching tabs and opening/closing detail screens does not cause ANR or crash
- [ ] **Portrait-only layout**: verify no layout overflow or clipping on a standard phone viewport
</content>
</page>

- [x] In the player app, in Library section, Genres are not appearing. Genres should also appear along side other options (like recently added, artists, songs, albums are all visible in library)
- [x] Show inflated number of listeners and followers for now - randomly add some random number in the range \[1000-2000\] to each number of followers/listeners to songs/albums/artist
- [x] Staging currently doesn’t allow opening the item and seeing details - there should be an option to see of all the fields that are exposed while creating items.
- [x] Images are too large and high quality and take too long to load - implement an industry-grade compression algorithm so that image sizes are within industry-standard limits and they load fast.
- [x] Band option in Studio and linked artists on Band page in players
- [x] In the player app, in Library, the favorites functionality should stay as is. But the Saved functionality (that triggers from Add to Library) currently only displays artists, albums, or genres explicitly added to library. This kind of functionality is correct for favorites. but for saved, if a song is added to library, it’s respective artist, album, and genre should automatically reflect in saved artists, album, or genres. Similarly, when an album is added to library, it’s artist should also appear in library under saved artists; such that opening the implicitly added artists from the saved section only shows albums or songs of that artist which are saved; or opening an implicitly added album from the saved section should only display the songs that were explicitly saved. In clearer words, the hierarchy is artist\>album\>song. So, when a song is explicitly added to library, its album and its artists should also be implicitly added to library (or merged without duplication if already exist in library). Similarly, when an album is explicitly added to library, its artist(s) should also be implicitly added to library (or merged without duplication, if already exists). Explicitly added items should appear as is in the library, but implicitly added ones should only include the explicit items that triggered them.
- [x] Uploaded images must be cropped visually by adjusting an intuitive rectangle frame instead of the current implementation which is some bars that need to be adjusted - it’s extremely unintuitive and there is no instant feedback.
- [x] There are generic “Failed to create artist” errors in studio app UI - all errors should be descriptive.
- [ ] Feed UI reel like
